title: Performance test plan
description: Backing JSON Schema for the `performance-test-plan` artifact type so /gaia-val-validate can enforce structural invariants, not merely route the type. The performance-test-plan artifact is a prose-markdown document with a YAML frontmatter metadata block (grounded in the on-disk corpus instance `.gaia/artifacts/test-artifacts/strategy/performance-test-plan-2026-03-13.md` plus the /gaia-perf-testing SKILL Step structure). The shared validate-artifact-schema.sh helper extracts the frontmatter for *.md instances and validates it against THIS schema; the prose body is not machine-validated by a frontmatter schema, so the seven canonical H2 sections it MUST carry are documented in `x-required-sections` below for the document-ruleset and reviewer. Pragmatic, grounded shape — required metadata is pinned, but additional frontmatter keys (e.g. `framework_version`) are permitted.

template.description: Template identifier. MUST be `performance-test-plan` — the slug that selects the document-specific ruleset and aligns with the on-disk artifact filename per the /gaia-val-validate artifact_type enum.

version.description: Template/document version (semver-shaped in the corpus, e.g. `1.0.0`). Bumped when the plan is re-run under a newer template. Note: quote in YAML frontmatter so a value like `1.0` is not parsed as a float by the validate-artifact-schema.sh YAML->JSON bridge.

date.description: Plan date. ISO-8601 `YYYY-MM-DD` per the corpus instance. Quote in YAML frontmatter (`date: "2026-03-13"`) so the validate-artifact-schema.sh YAML->JSON bridge does not load it as a non-serializable date object.

project.description: Human-readable project name the plan covers (e.g. `GAIA Framework`).

framework_version.description: Optional. The GAIA framework version at plan time, when recorded (present in the corpus instance). Informational provenance.

x-required-sections.description: The seven canonical H2 (`##`) sections the prose body MUST carry, grounded in the corpus instance + /gaia-perf-testing Step structure. In the corpus instance these H2 headings are numbered (e.g. `## 1. Overview`); the section list below is the unnumbered canonical name. Validated by the document-ruleset / reviewer, not by this frontmatter schema.
